description:
Canonical end-to-end system correctness dashboard for Chrono Queue. Proves that every job produced is fully accounted for: either successfully consumed by job-worker or persisted to the DLQ after exhausting retries. The fundamental invariant is: Produced = Consumed(success) + DLQ(persisted). Any deviation signals job loss, pipeline stall, or counter drift.

How to use: Check the Imbalance Threshold Alert (top row) first — green means balanced within tolerance. Drill into the Balance Range Totals to see absolute counts, then the Pipeline Delivery Status section to verify Kafka lag is draining. The Processing Activity section shows processing vs failure vs retry rates over time. Use the Loki cross-check row to validate Prometheus counters against log-line counts independently. Adjust the Imbalance Threshold variable to tune alert sensitivity for your SLO.

panels.description: Total jobs sent to Kafka by job-producer in the selected time range. Includes retry re-publishes from retry-dispatcher if any.
panels.title: Jobs Produced

panels.description: Alert-style indicator for balance drift. Green when produced jobs are fully accounted for, red when there is a positive unaccounted balance.
panels.title: Balance Drift Detected

targets.expr: round((sum(increase(chrono_jobs_produced_total{service="job-producer"}[$__range])) or vector(0)) - (sum(increase(chrono_jobs_consumed_total{service="job-worker", result="success"}[$__range])) or vector(0)) - (sum(increase(chrono_jobs_dlq_total{service="dlq-service"}[$__range])) or vector(0)))

panels.description: Percentage of produced jobs that are not yet accounted for by successful consumption or DLQ persistence over the selected time range.
panels.title: Unaccounted Jobs %

targets.expr: 100 * (((sum(increase(chrono_jobs_produced_total{service="job-producer"}[$__range])) or vector(0)) - (sum(increase(chrono_jobs_consumed_total{service="job-worker", result="success"}[$__range])) or vector(0)) - (sum(increase(chrono_jobs_dlq_total{service="dlq-service"}[$__range])) or vector(0))) / clamp_min((sum(increase(chrono_jobs_produced_total{service="job-producer"}[$__range])) or vector(0)), 1))

panels.description: Jobs successfully processed by job-worker in the selected time range.
panels.title: Jobs Consumed (Success)

panels.description: Jobs that exhausted all retries and were persisted to the DLQ in the selected time range.
panels.title: Jobs in DLQ

panels.description: Produced − (Consumed Success + DLQ). Should be 0 when all jobs are accounted for. A positive value means jobs are still in-flight or were lost. A negative value indicates counter drift (reset or double-counting).
panels.title: Unaccounted Jobs (Delta)

targets.expr: round((sum(increase(chrono_jobs_produced_total{service="job-producer"}[$__range])) or vector(0)) - (sum(increase(chrono_jobs_consumed_total{service="job-worker", result="success"}[$__range])) or vector(0)) - (sum(increase(chrono_jobs_dlq_total{service="dlq-service"}[$__range])) or vector(0)))

panels.description: Primary on-call signal. Fires ALERT (red) when the absolute job balance delta exceeds the configured imbalance threshold (currently ${imbalance_threshold} jobs). A threshold breach means more jobs are unaccounted for than the tolerance allows — investigate consumer lag, DLQ routing, or job-worker restarts. Tune the threshold variable to match your SLO. Green = system is balanced within tolerance.
panels.title: Imbalance Threshold Alert (≥ ${imbalance_threshold} jobs)

targets.expr: clamp_min(round((sum(increase(chrono_jobs_produced_total{service="job-producer"}[$__range])) or vector(0)) - (sum(increase(chrono_jobs_consumed_total{service="job-worker", result="success"}[$__range])) or vector(0)) - (sum(increase(chrono_jobs_dlq_total{service="dlq-service"}[$__range])) or vector(0))) - ${imbalance_threshold}, 0)

panels.description: Percentage of produced jobs that were fully resolved — either processed successfully by job-worker or persisted to the DLQ after exhausting retries. Should be 100% within any complete time window. A value below 99% means jobs are still in-flight, stuck in consumer lag, or were lost. SLO target: 100% resolution rate.
panels.title: Job Resolution Rate (SLO)

targets.expr: 100 * ((sum(increase(chrono_jobs_consumed_total{service="job-worker", result="success"}[$__range])) or vector(0)) + (sum(increase(chrono_jobs_dlq_total{service="dlq-service"}[$__range])) or vector(0))) / clamp_min((sum(increase(chrono_jobs_produced_total{service="job-producer"}[$__range])) or vector(0)), 1)

panels.description: Job counts per scrape interval using increase() — shows absolute job volume trend rather than per-second rate. Produced should broadly track Consumed+DLQ'd in steady state. A persistent gap between Produced and (Consumed+DLQ) means jobs are still in-flight or stuck in Kafka lag.

targets.expr: (sum(increase(chrono_jobs_produced_total{service="job-producer"}[$__rate_interval])) or vector(0)) - (sum(increase(chrono_jobs_consumed_total{service="job-worker", result="success"}[$__rate_interval])) or vector(0)) - (sum(increase(chrono_jobs_dlq_total{service="dlq-service"}[$__rate_interval])) or vector(0))

panels.title: Produced vs Consumed vs DLQ — Count Trend
Pipeline Delivery Status — Are All Jobs Consumed?

panels.description: Consumer lag across all job topics for job-worker. A lag of 0 means all produced Kafka records have been picked up (though not necessarily committed to success/DLQ yet). Non-zero lag means records are still queued.
panels.title: Worker — Max Consumer Lag

panels.description: Consumer lag for the DLQ service. A lag of 0 means all failed jobs pushed to the DLQ topic have been consumed and persisted to MongoDB.
panels.title: DLQ Service — Max Consumer Lag

panels.description: All Kafka records are fully processed when both worker lag AND DLQ lag are 0 simultaneously.
panels.title: Total Lag (Worker + DLQ)

panels.description: Running rate of jobs produced vs successfully consumed vs routed to DLQ. In steady state the consumed+DLQ lines should track the produced line.
panels.title: Job Flow Rate — Produced vs Consumed vs DLQ

panels.description: Consumer lag over time per job topic for job-worker. Should converge to 0 once production stops.
panels.title: Consumer Lag by Topic — job-worker

panels.description: Breakdown of produced, consumed, and DLQ'd jobs per job type within the selected range.
panels.title: Produced vs Consumed vs DLQ by Job Type (Range)

panels.description: Log-line counts from Loki to cross-verify the Prometheus counters. All three totals should satisfy: produced ≈ success + DLQ.
panels.title: Log-Based Job Counts (cross-check with Prometheus counters above)

targets.expr: sum(count_over_time({job="dlq"} |= "Failed job persisted to DLQ repository" [$__range]))

panels.title: job-worker — Jobs Processed vs Failed
panels.description: Log-based rate of jobs processed, failed, and scheduled for retry by job-worker.

panels.title: retry-dispatcher Requeue & Polling Rate
panels.description: Log-based rate of successful requeues and retry polling by retry-dispatcher.

panels.title: Job Flow: Produced vs Worker Consumed vs DLQ
panels.description: Compares the rate of jobs published by job-producer with the rate consumed by job-worker (first attempt + retries) and the rate landing in the DLQ. A rising DLQ line or growing gap between produced and consumed indicates a problem.

list.description: Minimum absolute job delta that triggers the Imbalance Threshold Alert panel. Lower values catch small losses early; higher values reduce noise during normal in-flight lag.
list.label: Imbalance Threshold (jobs)
list.name: imbalance_threshold

title: Chrono Queue — End-to-End System Correctness
